title: Lemma A: Reduce (i)-(iv) to standard SRBM with reflection matrix R=(I+G)^{-1}; handle det(I+G)=0

comments.author: human
comments.body:
Equation (i) is $W^\ast(t)=\xi^\ast(t)-G W^\ast(t)+Y^\ast(t)$, i.e. $(I+G)W^\ast(t)=\xi^\ast(t)+Y^\ast(t)$.

**To prove:** If $I+G$ is invertible, set $R:=(I+G)^{-1}$ and $X^\ast:=R\xi^\ast$. Then conditions (i)-(iv) are equivalent to the standard semimartingale reflecting Brownian motion (SRBM) data form
$$W^\ast(t)=X^\ast(t)+R\,Y^\ast(t),\quad W^\ast\ge0,\ Y^\ast\ \text{nondecreasing},\ \int_0^\infty W^\ast_j\,dY^\ast_j=0,$$
where $X^\ast$ is a BM with drift $-R\theta$ and covariance $R\Gamma R^\top$, and $R$ is the reflection matrix (its $j$-th column is the reflection direction on face $\{w_j=0\}$).

**Edge case (must not be skipped):** When $-1\in\mathrm{spec}(G)$, $\det(I+G)=0$. Then $W\mapsto(I+G)W$ is not invertible; analyze whether a solution can exist at all and whether $W^\ast$ is determined by $\xi^\ast+Y^\ast$. This degenerate case changes the existence/uniqueness conclusion and is easy to overlook.
